sentence: شْنُوَّة أَحْوَالَك؟ لَبَاس عْلِيك الْيُوم؟
raw: شنوة أحوالك؟ لباس عليك اليوم؟
ipa: ˈʃnuwwa ʔaħˈwɛːlak laˈbɛːs ˈʕliːk alˈjuːm
gloss_en: How are you? Are you well today?
notes: šnuwwa interrogative (Tunis hallmark; PADIC/QADI-TN attest); labās (Maghrebi greeting); geminate وّ; Tunis consonant system and lexicon (šnuwwa, gemination) per (singer1984)

sentence: اَلْجَوّ بَاهِي الْيُوم وَالْبْحَر هَادِي فِي تُونِس.
raw: الجو باهي اليوم والبحر هادي في تونس.
ipa: alˈʒaww ˈbɛːhiː lˈjuːm ˈwalbħar ˈhɛːdiː ˈfiː ˈtuːnis
gloss_en: The weather is fine today and the sea is calm in Tunis.
notes: bāhi 'good' (Tunisian hallmark lexeme); ج=/ʒ/ (singer1984); cluster lbḥ-

sentence: مَا نْحِبّْش قَهْوَة، نْحِبّ تَاي سْخُون بَرْشَة.
raw: ما نحبش قهوة، نحب تاي سخون برشة.
ipa: ˈmɛː ˈnħibbʃ ˈqahwa ˈnħibb ˈtaːj ˈsxuːn ˈbarʃa
gloss_en: I don't want coffee, I like very hot tea.
notes: n- 1sg + ma-...-š negation (Maghrebi, versteegh-type; singer1984); barša 'a lot' (Tunisian hallmark); geminate بّ

sentence: شْرِيت ثْلَاثَة كِيلُو عْنَب مِن السُّوق بِخَمْسَة دِينَارَات.
raw: شريت ثلاثة كيلو عنب من السوق بخمسة دينارات.
ipa: ˈʃriːt ˈθlɛːθa ˈkiːluː ˈʕnab ˈmin asˈsuːq biˈxamsa diːnaːˈraːt
gloss_en: I bought three kilos of grapes from the market for five dinars.
notes: ث retained /θ/ in θlāθa (gibson2009 EALL: Tunis interdental retention — spec reflexes reordered accordingly); ق=/q/ sūq; clusters šr-/θl-/ʕn-

sentence: اَلرَّاجِل هَاذَاكَة يِخْدِم فِي الظّهْر زَادَة.
raw: الراجل هاذاكة يخدم في الظهر زادة.
ipa: arˈraːʒil hɛːˈðɛːka ˈjixdim ˈfiː ˈɑðˤðˤhr ˈzɛːda
gloss_en: That man works at noon too.
notes: hāðāka demonstrative with ذ /ð/ retained (gibson2009); zāda 'also' (Tunisian); ظ=/ðˤ/ retained

sentence: اِمْشِي لِلسُّوق وْشْرِي الْخُبْز، يَعَيْشِك.
raw: امشي للسوق وشري الخبز، يعيشك.
ipa: ˈʔimʃiː lisˈsuːq ˈwʃriː lˈxubz jaˈʕiːʃik
gloss_en: Go to the market and buy the bread, please.
notes: Tunis imperatives imshi/shri with syncope clusters; yaʕayshik politeness with the cited ay→iː monophthongization (gibson2009)

sentence: الْيُوم الْجَوّ مُوش بَاهِي، فَمَّة بَرْد بَرْشَة.
raw: اليوم الجو موش باهي، فمة برد برشة.
ipa: alˈjuːm alˈʒaww ˈmuːʃ ˈbɛːhiː ˈfamma ˈbard ˈbarʃa
gloss_en: Today the weather isn't good, there's a lot of cold.
notes: mūš predicate negation + famma existential (Tunis; singer1984); bāhi 'good' and barša 'a lot' Tunis hallmark lexemes (gibson2009 EALL); imāla raises bāhi→[bɛːhiː].

sentence: بِقَدَّاش هَاذِي؟ بِثْلَاثَة دِينَار وْنُصّ.
raw: بقداش هاذي؟ بثلاثة دينار ونص.
ipa: biqadˈdɛːʃ ˈhɛːðiː biθˈlɛːθa diːˈnaːr ˈwnusˤsˤ
gloss_en: How much is this? Three dinars and a half.
notes: bqaddāš 'how much' (Tunis); ث retained /θ/ in θlāθa (gibson2009 EALL: Tunis interdental retention); imāla raises θlāθa→[θlɛːθa] but is blocked before /r/ in dinār→[diːnaːr] (singer1984 imāla-block).

sentence: وَقْتَاش الْمِيعَاد؟ عَ الْعَشْرَة الصّْبَاح.
raw: وقتاش الميعاد؟ ع العشرة الصباح.
ipa: waqˈtɛːʃ almiːˈʕaːd ˈʕa lˈʕaʃra sˤsˤˈbaːħ
gloss_en: When is the appointment? At ten in the morning.
notes: waqtāš 'when' (Tunis interrogative); imāla blocked adjacent to /ʕ/ (mīʕād→[miːʕaːd]) and under emphasis (ṣbāḥ→[sˤsˤbaːħ]) — singer1984, gibson2009; sun-letter assimilation written on ص.

sentence: نُسْكُن فِي دَار كْبِيرَة مَعَ الْعَايْلَة فِي تُونِس.
raw: نسكن في دار كبيرة مع العايلة في تونس.
ipa: ˈnuskun ˈfiː ˈdaːr ˈkbiːra ˈmaʕa lˈʕaːjla ˈfiː ˈtuːnis
gloss_en: I live in a big house with the family in Tunis.
notes: residence frame: nuskun 'I live', dār 'house', ʕāyla 'family' (Tunis); dār→[daːr] imāla blocked before /r/ (singer1984), so the long vowel keeps its back quality against the raised default (gibson2009).

sentence: أَمْشِي دُغْرِي وْدَوَّرْ عَ الْيَسَار قُدَّام الْجَامِع.
raw: أمشي دغري ودور ع اليسار قدام الجامع.
ipa: ˈʔamʃiː ˈduɣriː ˈwdawwar ˈʕa ljaˈsaːr qudˈdɛːm alˈʒɛːmiʕ
gloss_en: Go straight and turn left in front of the mosque.
notes: imperative chain amšī/dawwar; ج=/ʒ/ sedentary Maghrebi reflex, no occlusive onset (watson2002 p.16); imāla raises jāmiʕ→[ʒɛːmiʕ] and quddām→[quddɛːm] (gibson2009); dawwar keeps its geminate /ww/.

sentence: الْمَاكْلَة الّي كْلِينَا الْبَارِح كَانِت بَاهْيَة بَرْشَة.
raw: الماكلة الي كلينا البارح كانت باهية برشة.
ipa: alˈmɛːkla ˈalliː ˈkliːnɛː alˈbaːriħ ˈkɛːnit ˈbɛːhja ˈbarʃa
gloss_en: The food we ate yesterday was very good.
notes: past evaluation: mākla 'food', bāriḥ 'yesterday', barša 'a lot' (Tunis); imāla raised in mākla→[mɛːkla], kānit→[kɛːnit], bāhya→[bɛːhja] but blocked before /r/ in bāriḥ→[baːriħ] (singer1984, gibson2009).

sentence: وِين مْشِيت الْبَارِح؟ مَا لْقِيتْش صَاحْبِك فِي الدّار.
raw: وين مشيت البارح؟ ما لقيتش صاحبك في الدار.
ipa: ˈwiːn ˈmʃiːt alˈbaːriħ ˈmɛː ˈlqiːtʃ ˈsˤɑːħbik ˈfiː dˈdaːr
gloss_en: Where did you go yesterday? I didn't find your friend at home.
notes: wīn 'where' + mā...š negation (Tunis; singer1984); ق=/q/ retained (lqīt); emphatic backing in ṣāḥbik→[sˤɑːħbik]; dār→[daːr] imāla blocked before /r/ (gibson2009).

sentence: قَدَّاش عَنْدِك مِن وْلَاد؟ عَنْدِي زُوز.
raw: قداش عندك من ولاد؟ عندي زوز.
ipa: qadˈdɛːʃ ˈʕandik ˈmin ˈwlɛːd ˈʕandiː ˈzuːz
gloss_en: How many children do you have? I have two.
notes: qaddāš 'how many'; zūz 'two' < *zawj by long-distance sibilant harmony (singer1984:9); imāla raises qaddāš→[qadˈdɛːʃ] and wlād→[wlɛːd] (gibson2009).

sentence: كَان تْجِي غُدْوَة، نَمْشِيوْ لِلسُّوق مَعَ بَعْضْنَا.
raw: كان تجي غدوة، نمشيو للسوق مع بعضنا.
ipa: ˈkɛːn ˈtʒiː ˈɣudwa namˈʃiːw lisˈsuːq ˈmaʕa ˈbaʕdˤnɛː
gloss_en: If you come tomorrow, we'll go to the market together.
notes: conditional kān 'if' + ġudwa 'tomorrow' (Tunis; singer1984); n-...-w 1pl imperfect namšīw; ق=/q/ retained in sūq; imāla raises kān→[kɛːn] (gibson2009).

sentence: الْبَارِح مْشِيت لِلسُّوق وْشْرِيت خُبْز وْزِيت وْطَمَاطِم.
raw: البارح مشيت للسوق وشريت خبز وزيت وطماطم.
ipa: alˈbaːriħ ˈmʃiːt lisˈsuːq ˈwʃriːt ˈxubz ˈwziːt wtˤɑˈmɑːtˤim
gloss_en: Yesterday I went to the market and bought bread, oil and tomatoes.
notes: past-narrative shopping: mšīt 'I went', šrīt 'I bought', ṭamāṭim 'tomatoes' (Tunis); bāriḥ→[baːriħ] imāla blocked before /r/; emphatic backing in ṭamāṭim→[tˤɑmɑːtˤim] (singer1984); sūq /q/ retained (gibson2009).

sentence: غُدْوَة بَاش نْسَافْرُوا لْصَفَاقُس بِالْكَار.
raw: غدوة باش نسافروا لصفاقس بالكار.
ipa: ˈɣudwa ˈbɛːʃ ˈnsɛːfruː lsˤɑˈfaːqus bilˈkaːr
gloss_en: Tomorrow we will travel to Sfax by bus.
notes: future particle bāš + ġudwa 'tomorrow' (Tunis; singer1984); kār 'bus' (Romance adstrate); imāla raised in bāš→[bɛːʃ], nsāfrū→[nsɛːfruː] but blocked under emphasis in ṣfāqus→[sˤɑfaːqus] and before /r/ in kār→[kaːr] (gibson2009).

sentence: عَيَّطْلِي فِي التِّيلِيفُون قَبْل مَا تْجِي، يَعَيْشِك.
raw: عيطلي في التيليفون قبل ما تجي، يعيشك.
ipa: ʕajˈjɑtˤliː ˈfiː ttiːliːˈfuːn ˈqabl ˈmɛː ˈtʒiː jaˈʕiːʃik
gloss_en: Call me on the phone before you come, please.
notes: ʕayyaṭ 'to phone/call' imperative, keeping its geminate /jj/; yaʕayšik politeness with the cited ay→iː monophthongisation → [jaʕiːʃik] (gibson2009 monophthong rule; singer1984); sun-letter assimilation written on ت.

sentence: عَسْلَامَة، شْنُوَّة أَحْوَالِك؟ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّه لَبَاس.
raw: عسلامة، شنوة أحوالك؟ الحمد لله لباس.
ipa: ʕasˈlɛːma ˈʃnuwwa ʔaħˈwɛːlik alˈħamdu ˈlillah laˈbɛːs
gloss_en: Hello, how are you? Praise God, fine.
notes: greeting adjacency pair: ʕaslāma / šnuwwa aḥwālik / lābās Tunis formulae (singer1984); šnuwwa keeps geminate /ww/; imāla raises ʕaslāma→[ʕaslɛːma], aḥwālik→[ʔaħwɛːlik], lābās→[laˈbɛːs] (gibson2009).

sentence: الّي فَات مَات، وْرَبّي يْسَهِّل عْلِيك.
raw: الي فات مات، وربي يسهل عليك.
ipa: ˈalliː ˈfɛːt ˈmɛːt ˈwrabbiː ˈjsahhil ˈʕliːk
gloss_en: What's past is past, and may God make it easy for you.
notes: closing proverb 'illī fāt māt'; imāla raises fāt→[fɛːt] and māt→[mɛːt] (singer1984, gibson2009); geminates rabbī, ysahhil kept.
